paper_title: Diatom Valve Three-Dimensional Representation: A New Imaging Method Based on Combined Microscopies

methods.description: This method involves creating 3D images and models of diatom valves by merging SEM images and digital holograms. The process includes image acquisition, alignment, and transformation to achieve a comprehensive 3D representation.

steps.description: Acquire a holographic image (DHM) and reconstruct amplitude and phase maps of the acquired hologram.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire holographic image and reconstruct amplitude and phase maps

steps.description: Associate SEM and DHM images to numerical matrices M and N, respectively, based on pixel size and gray scale values.
steps.searchable_summary: convert images to numerical matrices based on pixel size and gray scale

steps.description: Add constant values to the matrix of lower dimension to match the size of the matrices.
steps.searchable_summary: add constants to smaller matrix to match sizes

steps.description: Select four points for alignment: two in the DHM amplitude map and two in the SEM image.
steps.searchable_summary: select alignment points in DHM and SEM images

steps.description: Translate, rotate, and magnify one image to align with the other using the selected points.
steps.searchable_summary: apply translation, rotation, and magnification for image alignment

steps.description: Implement texture mapping of the phase map retrieved by DHM with the SEM image.
steps.searchable_summary: apply texture mapping to combine phase map and SEM image
